sections.heading: Interpretation
sections.title: Staff appointments for National Identity Card Unit
sections.summary: This section outlines the procedures for appointing staff to the National Identity Card Unit, including the authority to designate a head and staff members, and the power to employ contract staff under specific conditions. It establishes the framework for personnel management within the Unit.

requirements.text: The Secretary to Cabinet and Head of the Civil Service may designate a suitably qualified public officer or person to head the Unit
The Secretary to Cabinet and Head of the Civil Service may designate such public officers or persons as may be necessary to assist the Unit
The Secretary to Cabinet and Head of the Civil Service may employ on contract suitably qualified persons to assist the Unit

sections.source_quote: 2B. Staff of National Identity Card Unit (1) (a) The Secretary to Cabinet and Head of the Civil Service may, subject to the approval of the Public Service Commission, designate – (i) a suitably qualified public officer or person to head the Unit; (ii) such public officers or persons as may be necessary to assist the Unit. (b) The Secretary to Cabinet and Head of the Civil Service may employ on contract, on such terms and conditions as he may determine, such suitably qualified persons, as may be necessary to assist the Unit in the proper discharge of its functions.

sections.heading: Designation of office
sections.title: Data collection and processing must comply with Data Protection Act
sections.summary: This section states that all data collection and processing activities under the National Identity Card Act must comply with the Data Protection Act. It establishes that personal data, including biometric information, collected through this Act is subject to the broader Data Protection Act's requirements for data handling, privacy, and security.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates that all identity card data processing must follow Data Protection Act standards, which could impact cross-border data transfers if the Data Protection Act has international data transfer requirements.

requirements.text: The collection and processing of personal data, including biometric information, under this Act shall be subject to the provisions of the Data Protection Act

sections.source_quote: 12. Collection and processing of data to be subject to Data Protection Act The collection and processing of personal data, including biometric information, under this Act shall be subject to the provisions of the Data Protection Act.
sections.relevance_reason: Directly mandates compliance with Data Protection Act for biometric data

sections.heading: Interpretation
sections.title: Staff appointments for National Identity Card Unit
sections.summary: This section outlines the procedures for appointing staff to the National Identity Card Unit, including the authority to designate a head and staff members, and the power to employ contract staff under specific conditions. It establishes the framework for personnel management within the Unit.

requirements.text: The Secretary to Cabinet and Head of the Civil Service may designate a suitably qualified public officer or person to head the Unit
The Secretary to Cabinet and Head of the Civil Service may designate such public officers or persons as may be necessary to assist the Unit
The Secretary to Cabinet and Head of the Civil Service may employ on contract suitably qualified persons to assist the Unit

sections.source_quote: 2B. Staff of National Identity Card Unit (1) (a) The Secretary to Cabinet and Head of the Civil Service may, subject to the approval of the Public Service Commission, designate – (i) a suitably qualified public officer or person to head the Unit; (ii) such public officers or persons as may be necessary to assist the Unit. sections.heading: Duty to be registered
sections.title: Mandatory identity card registration for citizens
sections.summary: This section requires citizens of Mauritius to apply for an identity card within six months of turning 18, with exceptions for non-residents or those with serious health conditions. It specifies the documents required for application, including birth certificates, naturalization certificates, and biometric information collection.

requirements.text: Every citizen must apply for an identity card within six months of attaining 18 years of age
Non-residents may apply for an identity card within a period determined by the Head of the Unit
Persons unable to comply due to serious illness may apply for an identity card within a period determined by the Head of the Unit
Applicants must produce birth certificate or certificate of registration/naturalisation as a citizen
Applicants must produce other documents as required by the Head of the Unit
Applicants must allow fingerprints and other biometric information to be taken and recorded
Applicants must allow themselves to be photographed for the identity card
Applicants certified as unable to provide biometric information due to permanent infirmity may be exempt from biometric collection

sections.source_quote: 4. Duty to be registered (1) (a) Subject to paragraph (b), every person who is a citizen of Mauritius shall, within 6 months of attaining the age of 18 and on such form as may be prescribed, apply for an identity card at any office designated by the Head of the Unit. (b) Where a person – (i) is not a resident of Mauritius; or (ii) is unable to comply with paragraph (a) on account of serious illness, as certified by a medical practitioner in the public service, or on any other compelling and reasonable ground, he may apply for an identity card in such manner and within such period of attaining the age of 1:8 as the Head of the Unit may determine. (2) Every person who applies for an identity card shall — (a) produce his birth certificate or his certificate of registration or naturalisation as a citizen of Mauritius, as the case may be; (b) produce such other documents as the Head of the Unit may require; (c) allow his fingerprints, and other biornetric information about himself, to be taken and recorded; and (d) allow himself to be photographed, for the purpose of the identity card.
sections.relevance_reason: Biometric data collection and identity registration requirements

sections.heading: Change of residence
sections.title: Mandatory residence change notification
sections.summary: This section requires individuals who change their residence to notify the National Identity Card Unit within 28 days. It specifies that the notification must be submitted in the form prescribed by the Unit, ensuring timely updates to the identity card records.

requirements.text: Any person who changes residence must notify the National Identity Card Unit within 28 days
The change of residence notification must be submitted in the form prescribed by the Head of the Unit

sections.source_quote: 6. Change of residence (1) Where any person issued with an identity card changes his residence, he shall, within 28 days of the occurrence, notify the change at any office designated by the Head of the Unit or in such manner as may be prescribed. (2) Any change of residence reported under subsection (1) shall be in such form as may be prescribed.
sections.relevance_reason: Residence change notification affects personal data records

sections.heading: Collection and processing of data to be subject to Data Protection Act
sections.title: Data collection subject to Data Protection Act
sections.summary: This section explicitly states that all personal data, including biometric information, collected and processed under the National Identity Card Act must comply with the Data Protection Act. This ensures that identity card data handling adheres to broader privacy and data protection standards.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates that identity card data processing must follow Data Protection Act standards, which could impact cross-border data transfers if the Data Protection Act has international data transfer requirements.

requirements.text: The collection and processing of personal data, including biometric information, under this Act shall be subject to the provisions of the Data Protection Act

sections.source_quote: 12. Collection and processing of data to be subject to Data Protection Act The collection and processing of personal data, including biometric information, under this Act shall be subject to the provisions of the Data Protection Act.
sections.relevance_reason: Data protection compliance for identity data
